relations.note: Emphasis-device limit and bold budget together govern text emphasis discipline.
Both constrain which emphasis devices may be used and where.

quotes: more than ~15% of running body text in any section
Bold is for **hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration**. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.
Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).
Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, the generic name.

rules.intent: Reserve bold for genuine hierarchy and key takeaways, preventing emphasis inflation.
rules.rule_text: Bold is for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure. Always bold: callout text per the callout specs, statistical callout numbers, and the brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only). Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, or the generic name.

quotes: Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.
Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

rules.intent: Prevent over-stacked emphasis so hierarchy stays legible.
rules.rule_text: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string, except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

effect.reserved_for: hyperlinks (underline); study names, notes, and citations (italic)
effect.subject: underline_and_italic

quotes: No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.
No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

rules.intent: Keep underline reserved for links and italics reserved for citations/notes to avoid ambiguous emphasis.
rules.rule_text: No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.
